qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Arbitrary Python code execution via execute_python_file with command injection potential
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets Auto-GPT classic (execute_python_file in autogpt/commands/execute_code.py:33), which is a different codebase from the advisory. The advisory describes RCE via disabled BlockExecutionBlock in AutoGPT Platform (autogpt_platform/backend/backend/blocks/block.py and autogpt_platform/backend/backend/api/features/v1.py). These are fundamentally different architectures: Auto-GPT classic uses command-based execution through LLM prompts, while AutoGPT Platform uses a block-based graph execution system with REST APIs.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Command injection in execute_shell allows arbitrary system command execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets Auto-GPT classic (execute_shell in autogpt/commands/execute_code.py:113), which is a different codebase from the advisory. The advisory describes RCE via disabled BlockExecutionBlock in AutoGPT Platform (autogpt_platform/backend/backend/blocks/block.py and related API endpoints). These are different architectures: Auto-GPT classic's execute_shell is a command handler for LLM-generated actions, while the advisory's vulnerability is in the Platform's block execution REST API that bypasses disabled block checks.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: SSRF in browse_website allows access to internal services and cloud metadata
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets Auto-GPT classic (browse_website in autogpt/commands/web_selenium.py:93), which is a different codebase from the advisory. The advisory describes RCE via disabled BlockExecutionBlock in AutoGPT Platform, not SSRF. While both involve remote execution capabilities, they are in different architectures and different vulnerable code paths. The advisory is about block execution bypassing disabled checks in the Platform's REST API, not about SSRF in classic AutoGPT's web browsing functionality.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: None of the three qualifying vulnerabilities match the advisory-described vulnerability. The advisory (GHSA-r277-3xc5-c79v) describes a Remote Code Execution vulnerability in AutoGPT Platform where the block execution API endpoints (v1.py#L355-395 and external/v1/routes.py#L79-93) fail to check the disabled flag, allowing any authenticated user to execute the disabled BlockInstallationBlock. In contrast, all three findings target Auto-GPT classic (a different codebase): vuln_arbitrary_python_exec targets execute_python_file in autogpt/commands/execute_code.py:33, vuln_command_injection_shell targets execute_shell in autogpt/commands/execute_code.py:113, and vuln_ssrf_browse_website targets browse_website in autogpt/commands/web_selenium.py:93. These are fundamentally different architectures: Auto-GPT classic uses command-based execution through LLM-generated JSON responses, while AutoGPT Platform uses a block-based graph execution system with REST APIs. The advisory references autogpt_platform/backend paths, but the findings reference autogpt/ paths, confirming they are in different codebases.
evidence: PoC targets repository Auto-GPT v0.2.2 (classic), while advisory describes AutoGPT Platform block execution vulnerability
Advisory describes REST API endpoints (/blocks/{block_id}/execute) with authenticated users, while findings describe LLM command handlers triggered by prompt injection
